AUTH.PASSWORD_INVALID: Password must be 8+ chars, include uppercase, a digit, and a special character.
AUTH.FORGOT_INSTRUCTIONS: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
AUTH.FORGOT_SENT: If an account with that email exists, we've sent a password reset link. Please check your inbox.
AUTH.RESET_SUCCESS: Your password has been reset successfully. You can now sign in with your new password.

DIPLOMA.NO_FONTS: No custom fonts. Default Helvetica will be used.
DIPLOMA.ROBOTO_INFO: Roboto is bundled as the built-in default font — Czech characters (č, š, ž, ř, ů…) work automatically.
DIPLOMA.DEFAULT_FONT_NOTE: This font applies to all text items that have no explicit font assigned.
DIPLOMA.MOCK_NOTE: These values are only for preview and are not saved.
DIPLOMA.COORDS_NOTE: X and Y are percentages of page width/height
DIPLOMA.DRAG_HINT: Drag items on the preview to reposition them

LANDING.HERO_SUBTITLE: The all-in-one platform for organizing sports events, scoring participants, and generating instant leaderboards and diplomas.
LANDING.FEATURES_SUBTITLE: From importing participants to printing diplomas — all in one place.
LANDING.FEATURE_IMPORT_DESC: Import participants and groups from a spreadsheet in seconds with smart column mapping.
LANDING.FEATURE_AI_DESC: Snap a photo of a score sheet and let Gemini AI extract the results automatically.
LANDING.FEATURE_LEADERBOARD_DESC: Real-time rankings by age category and gender, with CSV export.
LANDING.STEP1_DESC: Upload a CSV with participants, groups, and metadata. Map columns and you're set.
LANDING.STEP2_DESC: Assign evaluators to groups and score activities — manually or with AI scan.
LANDING.STEP3_DESC: See leaderboards instantly, export data, and generate custom diplomas.
LANDING.ORG_GROUPS_DESC: Organize participants into groups with age brackets and metadata.
LANDING.ORG_EVALUATORS_DESC: Build an evaluator pool per event and assign them to groups with drag & drop.
LANDING.ORG_DIPLOMAS_DESC: Design custom diploma templates and batch-generate PDFs for all winners.
LANDING.DOCS_SUBTITLE: Check out our step-by-step guides for admins and evaluators.
LANDING.DOCS_ADMIN_DESC: Learn how to create events, manage groups, assign evaluators, and generate diplomas.
LANDING.DOCS_EVAL_DESC: Learn how to score participants, use AI photo scanning, and work offline.
LANDING.CTA_BOTTOM_SUBTITLE: Create a free account and import your first event in minutes.

DOCS_ADMIN.TITLE: Admin Guide
DOCS_ADMIN.SUBTITLE: Everything you need to organize and manage your events
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_DESC: After logging in as an admin, you land on the dashboard. Here you'll see quick-action cards to create events or import from CSV, along with a list of all your events and their status. Use the cards to jump straight into event creation or management.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_DESC: There are two ways to create an event. You can use the manual form to enter event details one by one, or use the CSV import wizard to bulk-upload groups and participants at once. Both paths create a DRAFT event and redirect you to the 4-step setup wizard, where you configure groups, activities, evaluators, and then activate the event.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_DESC: After creating an event, you are redirected to the 4-step setup wizard. Step 1 — Groups: add groups and their participants. Step 2 — Activities: define activities and age brackets for the event. Step 3 — Evaluators: drag-and-drop evaluators from the event pool onto groups. Step 4 — Review & Activate: review your configuration and activate the event to make it live. While the event is in DRAFT status, you can revisit any step to make changes.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_DESC: The 3-step CSV import flow makes bulk event creation easy. Step 1: Upload your CSV file. Step 2: Map the CSV columns to the required fields (group name, participant name, external ID, gender, date of birth). The wizard auto-maps columns with matching names. Step 3: Preview the data and confirm the import.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_DESC: Groups and their participants are managed in Step 1 of the setup wizard while the event is in DRAFT status. Each participant has a name, external ID, gender, and date of birth. You can add, edit, or remove participants from groups at any time during setup. Once the event is activated, the event detail page shows groups in read-only mode.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_DESC: Evaluator assignment is a two-step process. First, assign evaluators to an event pool on the Admin > Evaluators page. Then, in Step 3 of the setup wizard, drag and drop pool evaluators onto specific groups. Each evaluator can only be assigned to one group per event, ensuring clear responsibility.
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_DESC: Activities define what participants are scored on and are configured in Step 2 of the setup wizard alongside age brackets. Each activity has a scoring type that determines how scores are entered and ranked:
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_NUMERIC_HIGH: Numeric High — Higher is better (e.g., distance, points)
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_NUMERIC_LOW: Numeric Low — Lower is better (e.g., time, errors)
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_SCORE_SET: Score Set — Predefined set of possible values
DOCS_ADMIN.AGE_CATEGORIES_DESC: Age brackets are configured in Step 2 of the setup wizard alongside activities. Define age brackets to automatically group participants on the leaderboard by age. Each category has a name, minimum age, and maximum age. Participants are assigned to categories based on their date of birth.
DOCS_ADMIN.LEADERBOARD_DESC: The leaderboard shows live rankings for each activity, filterable by gender and age category. Medals are displayed for the top three participants. You can export the full leaderboard as a CSV file for use in spreadsheets or official results publication.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_DESC: Design custom diplomas for your event. Upload a background image, add custom fonts, and position dynamic text items (participant name, activity, score, rank, event name, date) anywhere on the template. Use the live preview to see exactly how diplomas will look before generating them for participants.
DOCS_ADMIN.USERS_DESC: Manage all users in the system. Invite new evaluators by email — they'll receive a setup link to create their password. You can activate or deactivate accounts, change user roles, and view all registered users. Super admins have additional controls over admin accounts.

DOCS_EVAL.TITLE: Evaluator Guide
DOCS_EVAL.SUBTITLE: How to score participants quickly and accurately
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_DESC: When an admin invites you as an evaluator, you'll receive an email with a setup link. Click the link to set your password and activate your account. Once complete, you can sign in and start scoring.
DOCS_EVAL.DASHBOARD_DESC: Your dashboard shows all the groups you've been assigned to. Each card displays the group name, event name, and participant count. Click a group card to navigate to the event detail where you can start scoring.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_DESC: Select an activity to start scoring. You'll see a list of participants in your assigned group. Enter scores using the appropriate input for the activity type — number fields for numeric scores, toggles for boolean, or dropdowns for score sets. Save scores individually or use bulk save to submit all at once.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_SCORING_DESC: Speed up data entry by photographing handwritten score sheets. Upload a photo directly from the scoring view, and the AI will extract participant names and scores automatically. This works best with clearly written text and standard score sheet layouts.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_REVIEW_DESC: After the AI processes your photo, a review modal shows the extracted scores matched to participants. You can edit any value before confirming. Scores that the AI is less confident about are highlighted so you can double-check them. Only confirmed scores are saved.
DOCS_EVAL.OFFLINE_DESC: Scoring works even without an internet connection. When you're offline, scores are saved locally on your device. A pending indicator shows how many scores are waiting to sync. Once you're back online, scores are automatically uploaded to the server. No data is lost.
DOCS_EVAL.LEADERBOARD_DESC: Browse rankings by activity, gender, and age category. The top three participants receive medal indicators. You can download individual diplomas for winners directly from the leaderboard page.

AI_REVIEW.TITLE: Review AI Results
AI_REVIEW.SUBTITLE: Check the parsed scores and edit if needed before applying.
AI_REVIEW.NO_SCORES: No scores were recognized. Try a clearer photo.

SETUP.TITLE: Event Setup
SETUP.WARN_NO_GROUPS: No groups configured — add at least one group before activating.
SETUP.WARN_NO_ACTIVITIES: No activities configured — add at least one activity before activating.
SETUP.WARN_NO_EVALUATORS_POOL: No evaluators assigned to this event pool yet.
